name: Mulch Integration Pattern
description: Hybrid approach using programmatic API (@os-eco/mulch-cli) for record/search/query via dynamic import (avoids tsc resolving raw TS). CLI wrappers via Bun.spawn for prime, status, diff, learn, prune, doctor, ready, compact. Classification: foundational (tactical), observational. Record types: convention, pattern, failure, decision, reference, guide. Evidence tracking: commit, date, issue, file, bead. Outcomes: status (success/failure/partial), duration, test_results, agent, notes.

name: Canopy Integration Pattern
description: Pure CLI wrapper via Bun.spawn for all canopy operations. Wraps cn commands: render, validate, list, show. Always uses --json flag for structured output. Parses JSON responses from stdout. Error handling parses stderr for 'error:' lines. No programmatic API, all operations are CLI-driven.

content: OverstoryConfig has nested sections: project (name, root, canonicalBranch, qualityGates, defaultProfile), agents (manifestPath, baseDir, maxConcurrent=25, staggerDelayMs=2000, maxDepth=2, maxSessionsPerRun, maxAgentsPerLead=5), worktrees (baseDir), taskTracker (backend, enabled), mulch (enabled, domains, primeFormat), merge (aiResolveEnabled, reimagineEnabled), providers (Record of ProviderConfig), watchdog.

name: Agent Definition Layers
description: Two-layer architecture: Layer 1 (Base) — reusable .md files in agents/ defining the HOW (workflow, constraints, capabilities). Layer 2 (Overlay) — per-task CLAUDE.md generated by ov sling in worktree, defining the WHAT (task ID, file scope, spec path, branch). Base definitions use placeholders like {{AGENT_NAME}}, {{TASK_ID}}, {{INSTRUCTION_PATH}} (resolved at spawn time). Examples: scout.md (120 lines, read-only), builder.md (134 lines, implementation), lead.md (308 lines, coordination).

content: Mail store uses bun:sqlite synchronous API with WAL mode and 5000ms busy timeout for concurrent access. Schema: messages table with id (PK), from_agent, to_agent, subject, body, type (CHECK constraint), priority (CHECK low/normal/high/urgent), thread_id, payload (JSON), read (int boolean), created_at. Type values: status, question, result, error, worker_done, merge_ready, merged, merge_failed, escalation, health_check, dispatch, assign, decision_gate.

name: Merge Resolution Tiers
description: 4-tier escalation strategy in merge/resolver.ts: Tier 1 (Clean) — git merge with no conflicts. Tier 2 (Auto-resolve) — parse conflict markers, keep incoming (agent) changes. Tier 3 (AI-resolve) — use Claude to resolve remaining conflicts. Tier 4 (Re-imagine) — abort merge, reimplement from scratch. Each tier attempted in order; disabled tiers skipped. Pre-check: auto-commit os-eco state files (seeds/, overstory/, greenhouse/, mulch/, canopy/, CLAUDE.md) before attempting merge.

content: All subprocess execution uses Bun.spawn with consistent pattern: pass cmd array, cwd, stdout/stderr as 'pipe', await new Response(proc.stdout/stderr).text() for content, await proc.exited for exit code. Error handling throws typed errors (AgentError, MergeError, etc.) with context message including exit code and stderr.

Test philosophy: prefer real implementations over mocks. Use temp directories (mkdtemp) for filesystem tests, :memory: or temp files for bun:sqlite, real git repos for worktree/merge tests. Mock only when side effects are unacceptable (tmux, external API calls). Never use mock.module() — it leaks across test files. Tests colocated with source: module.test.ts alongside module.ts.

name: RPC Connection Abstraction
description: RuntimeConnection interface for headless agents (Pi, Sapling, etc.). Supports: followUp (send mail without tmux), abort (terminate agent), getState (poll ConnectionState: idle/working/error). Pi implements via JSON-RPC 2.0 over stdin/stdout. RpcProcessHandle provides stdin (write method) and stdout (ReadableStream). Event-driven agent health checks via watchdog daemon polling getState before evaluateHealth.

content: Error types hierarchy: OverstoryError base class (extends Error with name='OverstoryError'). Specific errors: AgentError, ConfigError, ValidationError, WorktreeError, MergeError, MailError. All thrown with typed message context. Command handlers use try-catch and jsonError() for JSON output, or throw for CLI stderr.

Dependencies: minimal runtime deps (chalk v5 ESM, commander.js, @os-eco/mulch-cli). External CLIs invoked as subprocesses (bd, sd, mulch, git, tmux). Dev deps: @types/bun, typescript, @biomejs/biome. NO npm AI SDK imports; runtimes abstract across Claude Code, Pi, Copilot, Gemini, etc. Dynamically import programmatic APIs to avoid tsc resolving raw .ts source.

Session/Run store uses bun:sqlite with WAL mode for concurrent access. AgentSession tracks: id, agent_name (unique), capability, worktree_path, branch_name, task_id, tmux_session, state (booting/working/stalled/done/failed), pid, parent_agent, depth, run_id, started_at, last_activity, escalation_level, stalled_since, transcript_path, prompt_version. Run tracks: id, started_at, completed_at, agent_count, coordinator_session_id, coordinator_name, status.

Guard rules are shared constants (pure data module) in src/agents/guard-rules.ts used for hook generation. Blocked for all agents: NATIVE_TEAM_TOOLS (Task, TeamCreate, etc.). Blocked for non-interactive: INTERACTIVE_TOOLS (AskUserQuestion, EnterPlanMode, EnterWorktree). Blocked for non-builders: WRITE_TOOLS (Write, Edit, NotebookEdit). Blocked DANGEROUS_BASH_PATTERNS: sed -i, redirects, rm/mv/cp, git add/commit/merge/push/reset/checkout, npm/bun install, runtime eval flags (-e, --eval, -c).

Event store uses bun:sqlite with WAL mode. Events table: id (autoinc PK), run_id, agent_name, session_id, event_type (tool-start, tool-end, mail-sent, error, etc.), tool_name, tool_args (JSON), tool_duration_ms, level (debug/info/warn/error CHECK), data (JSON), created_at (ISO 8601). Indexes on: agent_name+created_at, run_id+created_at, event_type+created_at, tool_name+agent_name, level WHERE error.

CLI uses Commander.js with version 0.8.7. 35 commands organized in src/commands/: init, sling, spec, prime, stop, status, dashboard, inspect, agents, clean, doctor, coordinator, supervisor, hooks, monitor, mail, merge, nudge, group, worktree, log, logs, watch, trace, feed, errors, replay, run, costs, metrics, ecosystem, update, upgrade, completions. Main entry point src/index.ts handles --version --json, sets project root override via --project global flag, handles errors with jsonError() for JSON output.
